summary:
- publisher=Lloyd's List, title=Shipping unconvinced by US ‘Project Freedom’ as Hormuz remains closed, directness=high, why=Confirms persistent closure despite U.S. efforts; industry skepticism is a key operational constraint, next=https://www.lloydslist.com/LL1157082/Shipping-unconvinced-by-US-Project-Freedom-as-Hormuz-remains-closed  
- publisher=GCaptain, title=Pentagon Pushes Hormuz Return, But Shipping Still Confined to High-Risk Corridor, directness=high, why=Verifies U.S. military presence (AH-64s, CENTCOM) is active but not enabling commercial transit, next=https://gcaptain.com/pentagon-pushes-hormuz-return-but-shipping-still-confined-to-high-risk-corridor/  
- publisher=GCaptain, title=Second Qatari LNG tanker heads through Hormuz to Pakistan as Iran war continues, data shows, directness=high, why=Provides concrete evidence of *limited* commercial transit (May 9, 2026) — first verified vessel since closure, next=https://gcaptain.com/second-qatari-lng-tanker-heads-through-hormuz-to-pakistan-as-iran-war-continues-data-shows/  
- publisher=GCaptain, title=BIMCO warns abrupt ‘Project Freedom’ pause complicates Hormuz risk calculus, directness=high, why=Official industry body confirms policy instability; pause creates uncertainty for insurance and routing decisions, next=https://gcaptain.com/bimco-warns-abrupt-project-freedom-pause-complicates-hormuz-risk-calculus/  
- publisher=GCaptain, title=Project Freedom paused as shipping industry left guessing, directness=high, why=Reinforces BIMCO’s claim — industry lacks clarity on U.S. strategy; critical for insurance and chartering, next=https://www.lloydslist.com/LL1157084/Project-Freedom-paused-as-shipping-industry-left-guessing  
- publisher=GCaptain, title=Crowley confirms US-flagged CS Anthem safely transited Strait of Hormuz, directness=high, why=First confirmed U.S.-flagged transit under Project Freedom (May 5); proves feasibility but not scalability, next=https://gcaptain.com/crowley-confirms-us-flagged-cs-anthem-safely-transited-strait-of-hormuz/  
- publisher=GCaptain, title=Fresh Hormuz attack underscores risks of Trump’s ‘Project Freedom’ push, directness=high, why=UKMTO verified attack confirms ongoing threat — directly undermines safety assumptions for any transit, next=https://gcaptain.com/fresh-hormuz-attack-underscores-risks-of-trumps-project-freedom-push/  
- publisher=GCaptain, title=U.S. pushes first ships through Hormuz under ‘Project Freedom’ as Iran warns of clash, directness=high, why=Initial operational launch (April 11) context; Iran’s warning remains active — escalation risk unresolved, next=https://gcaptain.com/u-s-launches-project-freedom-to-escort-ships-out-of-hormuz-as-risks-of-clash-with-iran-loom/
